title: The Middle English Charters of Christ
summary: The repository XML is a pinned copy of University of Michigan item AFW1075.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR and Level 1 TEI encoding of a sharply bounded selection from Mary Caroline Spalding's 1914 dissertation The Middle English Charters of Christ. Its header documents an immediate historical production layer of 230 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images: the XML contains 341 PB occurrences but only 230 unique REF filenames because parallel witness presentation reuses images. Current survival and retrievability of that TIFF set remain unknown. The encoding retains the title leaves, twelve complete Short Charter witness texts plus a separate Add Charter 5960 attestation block, seven Long Charter A-text witness streams, five B-text streams, and four Appendix II works; it omits the 124-page front matter, Appendix I, glossary, and residual back matter. Spalding's preface identifies four distinct transmission routes: collaborator transcripts, a Magdalen librarian's transcript, two earlier printed texts, and rotographs of all other printed manuscript passages. Complete public whole-codex facsimiles are cached for the Vernon manuscript and Bodleian MS Ashmole 61; complete scans of the 1914 edition, DIMEV v1.0, historical manuscript catalogs, current catalog records, and access documentation are also cached. No complete public facsimile was verified for the remaining source codices, and several historical/current foliation conflicts remain explicit.
review_notes: The cataloged artifact is an edition-level selection containing six abstract medieval works, not one medieval poem and not the complete 1914 dissertation.

Parallel witness streams are alternative textual witnesses, not separate intellectual works. Harley 6848 contributes variants and an attestation rather than a thirteenth complete Short Charter stream.
Long Charter C is discussed in omitted matter and is retained only as supporting context; it is not encoded as a continuous text.
Historical manuscript locators are preserved alongside current catalog locators where they conflict; none has been silently normalized.

The 230 historical U-M TIFF filenames constitute a distinct page-image carrier between the OCR encoding and Spalding's printed edition. The 341 PB occurrences are XML serialization events, not 341 separate images.

Underlying witness-text public-domain assertions are deliberately limited to United States law. United Kingdom status is left unresolved because official IPO guidance says some literary works unpublished at the end of 1988 remain protected through 31 December 2039.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afw1075
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AFW1075.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR text encoded at Level 1 under the TEI in Libraries guidelines, with links to 600 dpi page images. The public interface identifies the item as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States.

entities.label: Historical U-M page-image set for AFW1075.0001.001
entities.description: The page-image production layer from which U-M created its 2006 OCR. The XML header describes 230 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images, and its PB elements preserve all 230 unique filenames from 00000001.tif through 00000230.tif. The XML contains 341 PB occurrences because parallel witness presentation serializes some source pages more than once. No current image endpoint or complete retrievable bundle was verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Historically described as 230 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images
notes: The 230 unique REF values form a complete consecutive filename run, 00000001.tif through 00000230.tif; there are 341 PB occurrences because 62 source images are reused in the XML's parallel-witness serialization.
This entity records a historically evidenced production layer. Its unknown survival status means that current retrievability of the TIFF set has not been established.

entities.label: Mary Caroline Spalding, The Middle English Charters of Christ (1914)
entities.description: Spalding's Bryn Mawr dissertation and monograph. The encoded selection retains title leaves, edited witness texts on pp. 3-81, and Appendix II on pp. 95-102, while omitting the preface and study, Appendix I, glossary, and back matter.

bibliographic.citation: Mary Caroline Spalding, The Middle English Charters of Christ, Bryn Mawr College Monographs 15 (Bryn Mawr, PA: Bryn Mawr College, 1914).
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Charters of Christ

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Spalding 1914
entities.description: A complete public scan of the physical 1914 edition, with OCR and scan metadata.

entities.label: Carleton Brown working transcripts used by Spalding
entities.description: A modern scholarly working-copy intermediary, provisionally typed manuscript_witness because the schema has no transcript type. Brown supplied transcripts of Short Charter witnesses Sloane 3292, Stowe 620, and Harley 116 and the Long Charter B text in Cotton Caligula A II. Location, physical form, and survival are unknown.

entities.label: Alfred Rogers working transcripts used by Spalding
entities.description: A modern scholarly working-copy intermediary, provisionally typed manuscript_witness because the schema has no transcript type. Rogers supplied the Short Charter in St John's B.15, Carta Libera in E.24, and the Carta Celestis Hereditatis extract in CUL Ff.6.34. Location, physical form, and survival are unknown.

entities.label: H. A. Wilson transcript of Magdalen St Peter-in-the-East 18e
entities.description: A modern scholarly working transcript and manuscript description supplied to Spalding by the Magdalen College librarian, provisionally typed manuscript_witness because no transcript type exists. Location and survival are unknown.

entities.label: Spalding's manuscript rotographs
entities.description: A collective entity for rotographs of every printed manuscript text not routed through Brown, Rogers, Wilson, Macray, or Fehr. It is not evidence of whole-codex photography; each relation is scoped to the target passage. Location and survival are unknown.
entities.reproduction_format: Historical rotographs of selected manuscript passages

entities.label: B. Fehr's Fairfax Short Charter text
entities.description: The previously printed Fairfax/Add MS 5465 Short Charter text reprinted by Spalding rather than freshly transcribed from the codex.

bibliographic.citation: B. Fehr, Short Charter text from the Fairfax manuscript, Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 106 (1901), 69-70.

entities.label: W. D. Macray's Kent Charter text
entities.description: The earlier printed text of Bodleian Kent Charter 233 that Spalding reprinted as Carta Dei.

bibliographic.citation: W. D. Macray, Kent Charter text, Notes and Queries, 9th series, 8 (1901), 240.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 3292
entities.description: Short Charter witness printed by Spalding from Brown's transcript, at f. 2.

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 620
entities.description: Short Charter witness printed by Spalding from Brown's transcript, at ff. 11v-12r.

entities.label: British Library Add Charter 5960
entities.description: Short Charter base text with a separate prose attestation and seal/signature description in Spalding, pp. 6-7.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 6848
entities.description: Short Charter comparison witness at ff. 239v-240r; its readings are reported as variants to Add Charter 5960 and its attestation, not as a continuous thirteenth printed witness stream.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 37049
entities.description: Short Charter witness at f. 23r, dated by the current catalog to 1460-1500. The current viewer link reported unavailable images during this audit.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 116
entities.description: Short Charter witness at f. 97v, printed from Brown's transcript. The current viewer link reported unavailable images during this audit.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 24343
entities.description: Short Charter witness at ff. 6v-7r.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 230
entities.description: Fifteenth-century Short Charter witness at f. 25v, cataloged by James as manuscript entry 215 and associated with St Albans Abbey.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: Short Charter witness at f. 106r in a manuscript dated 1490-1510; the complete current digital object is public and cached.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 237
entities.description: Short Charter witness at f. 100r-v.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 5465 (Fairfax manuscript)
entities.description: Short Charter witness reprinted through Fehr. Spalding gives ff. 119r-122r; DIMEV's current row begins at f. 118, so the locator conflict remains open.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 189
entities.description: Short Charter witness in the second physical part. Spalding's XML milestones span ff. 109r-110r; the current catalog item identifies the Short Charter at f. 109, so both descriptions are retained.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS B.15
entities.description: Short Charter witness at f. 53r in the first, fifteenth-century unit of a composite medical manuscript; printed from Alfred Rogers's transcript.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson poet. 175
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 94v-95v. Spalding treated it as the oldest extant Long Charter witness, about 1350; the current catalog dates the codex to 1350-1400.

date_statements.label: Current catalog
date_statements.value: second half of the fourteenth century

entities.label: British Library Add MS 11307
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 89r-97r.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodl. 89
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 45r-49r, identified as Carta Christi in the current catalog and dated 1425-1450.

date_statements.label: Current catalog
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2346
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 51r-55r. The XML preserves an anomalous f. 53 then f. 52 milestone sequence rather than silently repairing it.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Add. C. 280
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness printed by Spalding from ff. 124r-125v. The current catalog item spans ff. 124-127, which may include additional neighboring material; both scopes are retained.

date_statements.label: Current catalog
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 5396
entities.description: Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 301r-305v.

entities.label: Magdalen College Oxford, St Peter-in-the-East 18e
entities.description: A 63-line Long Charter A-text fragment on the verso, printed from H. A. Wilson's supplied transcript and description. No exact current online catalog record or public complete facsimile was verified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon manuscript)
entities.description: Collateral Long Charter A-text witness at ff. 317v-318r, discussed and previously printed but not one of the seven continuous A-text streams encoded on pp. 18-44.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.3.26
entities.description: Long Charter B-text witness. Spalding/XML locates the text at ff. 235r-237r; DIMEV's current record uses ff. 238-240, an unresolved foliation crosswalk.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9
entities.description: Incomplete Long Charter B-text witness at ff. 42v-47r, beginning at source line 69.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II, part 1
entities.description: Long Charter B-text witness at ff. 77r-79r, printed from Carleton Brown's transcript.

holding.shelfmark: Cotton MS Caligula A II, ff. 3-139

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Long Charter B-text witness. Spalding/XML uses ff. 39v-42v; the current Database of Middle English Romance and DIMEV use ff. 47v-50v, an unresolved foliation crosswalk.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ee.2.15
entities.description: Long Charter B-text witness beginning at source line 69. Spalding/XML uses ff. 90r-94r; DIMEV's current row gives ff. 107-111, an unresolved foliation crosswalk.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 C XVII
entities.description: Unique Long Charter C-text witness used for Spalding's classification and omitted Appendix I observations. It is not encoded as a continuous text in afw1075.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 21253
entities.description: Fifteenth-century Latin Carta Domini Nostri Iesu Christi at ff. 186r-v, printed in Appendix II.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS E.24
entities.description: Fourteenth-century base witness of the Latin verse Carta Libera at f. 22r, printed from Alfred Rogers's transcript.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS D.8
entities.description: Fifteenth-century comparison witness of Carta Libera under the title Carta Redempcionis humane at f. 174v; its readings are variants, including two witness-only lines.

entities.label: Bodleian Library Kent Charter 233
entities.description: The Middle English verse Carta Dei, written on the dorse of a legal conveyance dated 1395 and reprinted by Spalding through Macray. An exact current online catalog identifier and public complete facsimile were not verified.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.6.34
entities.description: Witness of the incomplete Middle English prose Carta Celestis Hereditatis extract at ff. 72r-74v, printed from Alfred Rogers's transcript and related by Spalding to The Poor Caitiff.

entities.label: Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: The complete 358-image Digital Bodleian object, cached at an 1800-pixel derivative profile; the Short Charter is f. 106r, canvas 229.

entities.label: Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of the Vernon manuscript
entities.description: The complete 710-image Digital Bodleian object, cached at a 1600-pixel derivative profile; the Long Charter occupies ff. 317v-318r, canvases 521-522.

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse v1.0
entities.description: Versioned XML release used to identify the Short Charter, Long Charter A/B/C, and Carta Dei traditions and current manuscript locators. Later witness lists are collateral scholarship, not retroactive sources of Spalding's edition.

entities.label: METS introduction to The Short Charter of Christ
entities.description: Modern scholarly introduction to the Ashmole 61 Short Charter, its genre, likely date, manuscript count, and relation to other charter poems.

entities.label: British Library current catalog records for afw1075 witnesses
entities.description: Aggregate of the exact current British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue records cached for the thirteen BL source codices and the Cotton part record.

entities.label: Bodleian current catalog records for afw1075 witnesses
entities.description: Pinned TEI records for Ashmole 61, Ashmole 189, Rawlinson poet. 175, Bodley 89, and Add. C. 280, plus current public catalog records and Digital Bodleian manifests.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library catalog descriptions for afw1075 witnesses
entities.description: Complete historical catalog volumes covering Ee, Ff, and Ii manuscripts, supplemented by current scholarly and institutional access pages.

entities.label: M. R. James catalog descriptions of St John's B.15, D.8, and E.24
entities.description: Complete 1913 catalog scan containing the exact three St John's witnesses and their target loci.

entities.label: M. R. James catalog description of Caius MS 230
entities.description: Complete 1907 catalog volume containing entry 215 for MS 230 and its Short Charter.

subject.description: Complete U-M encoding, including OCR, empty omitted-matter divisions, and repeated page-break serialization for parallel witnesses

object.description: All 230 unique historical TIFF page images represented by 341 PB occurrences

scope.notes: The item-specific header says that the OCR text was created with linked digital page images. The 341 PB occurrences do not imply 341 distinct image files; 62 images are reused in the parallel-witness serialization.

object.description: Complete Spalding 1914 printed edition as represented by source-image sequences 1-230

scope.notes: The continuous source-image sequence covers title leaves, preliminary matter, printed pp. 3-104, and residual leaves. The exact physical copy scanned and the TIFF set's current survival remain unknown.

subject.description: Sloane 3292, Stowe 620, Harley 116, and Cotton Caligula A II printed streams

object.description: Rotographs of Harley 6848 ff. 239v-240r and St John's D.8 f. 174v

access.access_method: No public copy of Spalding's historical rotograph set or complete public facsimiles for the remaining exact source codices was verified; use the provider request routes recorded above.

rights.component: 1914 text, editorial matter, and typography
rights.basis: Published in the United States in 1914; the title leaf names Bryn Mawr College as copyright holder, but the term has expired.

rights.component: U-M digital text and public delivery
rights.basis: The current U-M item identifies the materials as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not itself a license or worldwide public-domain determination.

The legacy 2006 XML header says 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and that other distribution restrictions may apply; that historical caution is preserved here rather than treated as overriding the current item-level NoC-US assertion.

rights.component: Historical U-M TIFF page-image production set for Spalding's 1914 edition
rights.basis: The current U-M item identifies its materials as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States, while the underlying 1914 edition is public domain in the United States. The historical TIFF set is not currently retrievable, and no separate image-specific license or delivery terms were verified.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not itself a license or worldwide public-domain determination.
The legacy XML says that, where applicable, copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply; that caveat remains material until U-M confirms TIFF-specific scope and current delivery terms.
This record does not infer an open license or current public image access from historical filenames.

rights.component: Pinned CME submodule XML input, including inherited U-M transcription and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE contains the Unlicense and dedicates 'software' to the public domain. It does not expressly establish that the repository has authority to dedicate inherited U-M XML, transcription, or markup, so the Unlicense assertion is recorded without treating it as conclusive for this data artifact.
notes: The current U-M item-level public-domain / NoC-US assertion is recorded separately on the digital-encoding entity.

editorial_practices.description: U-M states that OCR was uncorrected and content unedited; the text was encoded at TEI in Libraries Level 1 and linked to page images.

The encoding retains title leaves, printed pp. 3-81, and Appendix II pp. 95-102; it represents the 124-page front matter, Appendix I, and back matter only with empty page placeholders or omission divisions.
editorial_practices.representation: Explicit omitted-matter divisions and retained page images

editorial_practices.description: Spalding prints alternative Long Charter witnesses in parallel page columns; the OCR/TEI serializes those columns into consecutive witness DIV2 streams. They remain alternative witnesses, not one continuous composite poem.
editorial_practices.scope: Long Charter A and B texts, pp. 18-81
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel print columns serialized as separate witness streams

editorial_practices.description: Harley 6848 readings and attestation details are reported against Add Charter 5960 rather than printed as a thirteenth continuous Short Charter witness.
St John's E.24 supplies the Carta Libera base text and D.8 supplies variants, including two witness-only lines retained by the encoding.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned source XML for afw1075.
evidence.locator: header; title page; omitted-matter divisions; all retained DIV1/DIV2 streams
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the U-M identifier, 2006 OCR/TEI production statement, the historically linked 230-image 600 dpi TIFF G4 production layer, bibliographic source, selection boundary, witness headings, printed pages, manuscript folio milestones, notes, and textual content. The file is 241,848 bytes, SHA-256 a22aef6cae02d02326585ab7637e5f9b96798f845dc8f182df2991b5877716ca, and git blob c12d6ba523150f19c51346a47fd77d45efdc8eba.

evidence.citation: Local structural audit of afw1075.xml against the complete 1914 edition scan.
evidence.summary: Confirmed seven DIV1, 29 DIV2, 121 LG, 3,870 L, 25 P, 341 PB occurrences but exactly 230 unique consecutive REF filenames (00000001.tif-00000230.tif), and 110 manuscript-folio milestones; 62 source images recur because of parallel-witness serialization; title images 1-2; 124-page front-matter placeholder run; retained pp. 3-81 and 95-102; and omitted Appendix I and back matter.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, The Middle English charters of Christ, AFW1075.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Current official item record for title, author, publication, exact stable identifier, public-domain statement, DPLA No Copyright - United States status, and the table of contents matching the repository XML.

evidence.citation: Mary Caroline Spalding, The Middle English Charters of Christ (Bryn Mawr, PA: Bryn Mawr College, 1914).
evidence.locator: title and copyright leaves; preface pp. iii-iv; contents; texts pp. 3-81; Appendix II pp. 95-102
evidence.summary: Immediate print source and primary evidence for edition title, editor, copyright notice, publication, intellectual boundaries, manuscript sigla and loci, witness descriptions, text families, apparatus, and exact encoded/omitted page boundaries.

evidence.citation: Spalding 1914, preface, pp. iii-iv.
evidence.summary: Direct method statement: Macray supplied the Kent Charter print; Fehr the Fairfax print; Brown supplied four transcripts; Rogers supplied three transcripts; Wilson supplied the Magdalen fragment transcript and description; all other printed texts depended directly on rotographs of original manuscripts.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete Internet Archive Spalding scan and derivative files.
evidence.summary: Verified PDF integrity, 244 PDF pages, complete physical-edition coverage, title/copyright leaves, all encoded text boundaries, and representative pages; OCR, metadata, and scan-data checksums were also recorded.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 6769, 106.5, 2859, 6650, 1913, and 3012 and linked manuscript/bibliography rows.
evidence.locator: Records.xml; Manuscripts.xml; MSSIndex.xml; Bibliography.xml; README.md; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for modern work identities, NIMEV numbers, broader witness counts, current manuscript loci, edition bibliography, and the CC BY 4.0 release license. It distinguishes the Fairfax carol version and Long Charter A, B, and C records.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Introduction to Item 29, The Short Charter of Christ.
evidence.locator: Origin, Genre, and Themes; Manuscript Context; Text; Printed Editions
evidence.summary: Supports a likely fifteenth-century Short Charter date, legal/devotional/Passion genre, relationship but non-identity with Carta Dei and the Long Charter, survival in twenty-four manuscripts, Ashmole 61's four-line ending omission, and the later critique of Spalding's Ashmole 61/Harley 237 grouping.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford catalog records and pinned TEI for MSS Ashmole 61, Ashmole 189, Rawlinson poet. 175, Bodley 89, Add. C. 280, and the Vernon manuscript.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmarks, dates, physical parts, target items, current loci, Digital Bodleian object identifiers, and the absence of surrogate links in several exact TEI records.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete Bodleian MS Ashmole 61 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verified manifest/inventory presence, 358-image completeness, ZIP integrity, representative images, target f. 106r/canvas 229, checksum, and the embedded CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution statement.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete Vernon manuscript IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verified manifest/inventory presence, 710-image completeness, ZIP integrity, target Long Charter folios/canvases, checksum, and the embedded CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution statement.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue records for the afw1075 source witnesses.
evidence.summary: Exact current records support shelfmarks, dates, physical part boundaries, target loci where itemized, and current request links. All exact record HTML files are cached; the Add 37049 and Harley 116 linked viewers reported unavailable images during the audit.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vols. 2 and 4 (1857-1861), with current scholarly manuscript descriptions.
evidence.summary: Historical and current evidence for the Ee.2.15, Ff.2.38, Ff.6.34, Ii.3.26, and Ii.4.9 codices, including target content and unresolved historical/current foliation conflicts.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of St John's College, Cambridge (1913), MSS 37/B.15, 83/D.8, and 127/E.24.
evidence.locator: B.15 Short Charter f. 53; D.8 Carta Redempcionis f. 174v; E.24 Carta Libera f. 22
evidence.summary: Complete catalog evidence for the three St John's source witnesses, manuscript dates, physical descriptions, target titles, and loci.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (1907), entry 215, MS 230.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 252 ff.; entry 215 / MS 230; Short Charter f. 25v
evidence.summary: Supports the current shelfmark form MS 230, fifteenth-century date, St Albans connection, contents, and Short Charter locus.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections copying and imaging services.
evidence.summary: Current official route for requesting images of source manuscripts without public complete digital objects.

evidence.citation: British Library digitisation services and current physical-item request route.
evidence.summary: Current route for digitisation enquiries and physical consultation; no open reuse grant for prospective supplied images was verified.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Cultural Heritage Imaging Laboratory.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for ordering high-quality images and licensing their reproduction.

evidence.citation: St John's College Cambridge Library, consultation and reproduction guidance.
evidence.summary: States that Special Collections material is supervised, reading or high-resolution copies may be requested, and college permission is required for reproduction in publication, websites, or presentations.

evidence.citation: Gonville and Caius College library page.
evidence.summary: Current institutional contact route for enquiries concerning the manuscript collection; exact imaging availability and terms for MS 230 require confirmation.

evidence.citation: Magdalen College Oxford, Access to the Archives and Archives Reprographics.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for archive enquiries, onsite consultation, small remote image requests, fee-based larger photography, and required college permission for publication.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (term).
evidence.locator: Pre-1989 unpublished works (the '2039' rule); anonymous or pseudonymous works
evidence.summary: Official guidance states that some literary works unpublished at the end of 1988 remain protected through 31 December 2039 even when created centuries ago, and that anonymous-work analysis depends on reasonable assumptions about authorship and expiry. This prevents a blanket United Kingdom public-domain assertion for every manuscript witness without item-specific publication-history research.

evidence.citation: Dated public-access sweep for complete digital facsimiles of all afw1075 source codices.
evidence.summary: Checks of exact institutional catalogs, linked viewers, IIIF services, DIMEV/manuscript indexes, and exact-shelfmark searches verified complete public objects only for Ashmole 61 and Vernon. Add 37049 and Harley 116 expose viewer links whose images were unavailable. This is a dated negative public-access finding, not a claim that the remaining manuscripts have never been digitized privately.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, pinned root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The pinned repository root contains the Unlicense text, which repeatedly describes the dedicated subject as 'software.' This establishes the repository-level assertion but does not expressly settle authority or scope for inherited U-M XML, transcription, or markup.

open_questions.question: Do the historical 230 U-M TIFF G4 page images still survive, and is there a current supported complete-set delivery or request route?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections about AFW1075.0001.001 and the exact filename range 00000001.tif-00000230.tif.
Request the complete 230-image set rather than only the target text pages if it remains available.
Record any current endpoint, inventory, delivery terms, and TIFF-specific rights statement only after verification.

open_questions.question: Can the holding institutions supply complete current digital facsimiles of the remaining exact source codices, and under what reuse terms?
next_steps: Use the recorded institutional routes and request whole physical objects rather than target crops when practical.
Identify the target folios separately in each request.
Record quote, delivery format, image resolution, and publication/repository terms verbatim.

open_questions.question: How do Spalding's historical folio references crosswalk to current catalog foliation for Ashmole 189, Add. C. 280, CUL Ii.3.26, Ff.2.38, and Ee.2.15?
next_steps: Ask each repository for a current foliation map or inspect complete current images.
Preserve both historical and current locators until the physical sequence is verified.
Check whether the differences reflect rebinding, missing leaves, or catalog scope rather than transcription error.

open_questions.question: Do Spalding's historical rotographs or the Brown, Rogers, and Wilson working transcripts survive in an identifiable archive?
next_steps: Search Bryn Mawr College archives and relevant personal papers.
Do not infer whole-manuscript coverage from the word rotograph.
If found, inventory each research carrier and connect it to the exact witness passage.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current shelfmark and custodial description of Magdalen College St Peter-in-the-East 18e?
next_steps: Email Magdalen Archives with Spalding's shelfmark, the verso fragment incipit, and H. A. Wilson's 1912-era description.
Ask whether the fragment has been rebound, renumbered, transferred, or digitized.

open_questions.question: What exact current Bodleian catalog identifier and reproduction route correspond to Kent Charter 233?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Special Collections to crosswalk the historical charter shelfmark.
Request the complete physical charter, including recto conveyance, dorse poem, seals, endorsements, and scale target.

open_questions.question: Were all source-witness texts validly made available to the public before the United Kingdom's pre-1989 unpublished-work cutoff, or could any remain protected through 31 December 2039?
next_steps: Determine the authorized publication history of each exact witness text rather than equating manuscript age with United Kingdom public-domain status.
Seek institution-specific rights guidance before first publication of a witness text or supplied manuscript image in the United Kingdom.
Keep the recorded witness-text public-domain assertions limited to United States law until resolved.

open_questions.question: Should the Short Charter be dated to the late fourteenth century, as Spalding argued from linguistic and documentary evidence, or probably a century after the early-fourteenth-century analogues, as the modern METS introduction proposes?
next_steps: Compare the full modern bibliography and manuscript stratigraphy rather than selecting one date silently.
Keep composition date separate from dates of surviving witnesses.
